session.name: MPI-8 verification + Windows portable launch validation

goal.original: Continue MPI-8: verify committed icon/build-hash/portable slice, then launch the Windows portable artifact and run platform validation.
goal.summary: Committed slice verified. Verification found and fixed two real build-portable.mjs defects (dev-junk/agent-doc payload leak; recursive-copy bomb when staging inside the repo). A fresh clean Windows artifact launches from the staged Electron and the server reaches 127.0.0.1:3000 with portable roots honored. Fix committed in 9246b3a. MPI-8 stays in doing/needs_verification for deeper Windows checks and Linux/macOS validation.

completed: Verified committed slice on disk (icons, js/core/buildInfo.js=dev, scripts/portable/apply-update.cjs).
Fixed recursive-copy bomb: skipAbs walker guard + fail-fast in-repo stage-dir refusal + D:\tmp allowance in assertSafeClean.
Built fresh clean Windows artifact to D:\tmp\cubric-validate (buildHash fea34e40c89c, 5330 files); verified clean app/ payload.
Launched from staged Electron: server reached 127.0.0.1:3000, portable user-data root honored, GPU/engine config resolved, GET / + /system/stats + /system/platform-config OK.
Verified build-hash error-report logic in routes/system.js by read (no GitHub issue POSTed).
Committed fix + package.json build:portable:win|linux|mac aliases + project-profile entry in 9246b3a.
pending: Deeper Windows validation: engine install/repair, Models slide-over discovery, one image generation, restart persistence, folder-open via Electron bridge, video extraction/crop, a live error-report submission, update.bat + update-from-zip.bat on a copied portable folder.
Linux artifact extraction/launch + engine bootstrap on the Ubuntu laptop (generation stays community/stronger-host validated).
macOS staging stays maintainer-untested; needs contributor validation.

task_card.title: Portable distribution, updater, and release validation

completed: MPI-8 plan.md Plan Drift, validation.md, and events.jsonl record the fix + Windows launch validation.
Fix and discoverability changes committed in 9246b3a on master.
File claim ed3a4377 (scripts/build-portable.mjs) set to verified.
pending: Run the deeper Windows interactive checks listed in plan.md Phase 4 / Verification.
Do not move MPI-8 to done until platform validation is represented and explicitly approved.
Ask before updating .claude/rules/ if later validation changes component wiring, workspace routing, events, state, or Comfy injection behavior.

next_action.description: Continue MPI-8: a good Windows artifact already exists at D:\tmp\cubric-validate\CubricVision-windows-x64-v0.0.1\ (double-click start.bat to launch). Decide with the user which deeper Windows checks the agent can self-verify vs. which the user verifies on the installed build, then proceed; afterward move to Linux validation on the Ubuntu laptop.

key_decisions: Single build-portable.mjs covers win32/linux/darwin via PLATFORM_CONFIG; not separate per-platform scripts.
Stage to D:\tmp (C: is space-constrained). Script refuses staging inside the repo tree except under dist/.
ELECTRON_RUN_AS_NODE=1 is set in the Claude Code shell; electron.exe must be launched with `env -u ELECTRON_RUN_AS_NODE` from this environment. A user double-clicking start.bat is unaffected.
Build-hash label (build:<hash>) is added only for a real non-dev hex hash; dev/absent normalize to no label.
constraints: No Git requirement for user updates; updater uses release assets/manifests.
macOS stays maintainer-untested until community validation.
Linux generation stays unvalidated on the weak Ubuntu laptop.
MPI-8 stays in doing; do not close without explicit approval.
NEVER commit unless the user asks.

resume_prompt: Read .agents/mpi-kanban/state/handoffs/63e9bb90-f2b5-441f-a949-978a7385abcc.json and use mpi-continue to continue MPI-8. The committed slice and a build-portable.mjs fix (commit 9246b3a) are verified; a good Windows artifact exists at D:\tmp\cubric-validate\CubricVision-windows-x64-v0.0.1\ (start.bat). Next: agree with the user which deeper Windows checks to self-verify vs. user-verify on the installed build, run them, then move to Linux validation on the Ubuntu laptop. In this shell, launch electron with `env -u ELECTRON_RUN_AS_NODE`.
